steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: For coordinate-circle questions, read the center and radius from the equation or graph the circle as a check. For diagram facts, mark equal radii, diameters, tangents, and arcs before doing algebra.

steps.title: Use unit circle coordinates to find sine and cosine
steps.content:
On a unit circle, a point at angle $\theta$ from the positive $x$-axis has coordinates $(\cos\theta, \sin\theta)$.

Point $A = (1,0)$ is at angle $0$. Point $B = \left(\frac{5}{\sqrt{34}}, -\frac{3}{\sqrt{34}}\right)$ is on the unit circle.

If angle $p$ is the angle from ray $OA$ to ray $OB$ (counterclockwise positive), then $B$ is located at angle $p$, so by the unit circle property:
$$\cos p = \frac{5}{\sqrt{34}} \quad \text{and} \quad \sin p = -\frac{3}{\sqrt{34}}$$

steps.title: Divide to find the quotient
steps.content:
$$\frac{\cos p}{\sin p} = \frac{\frac{5}{\sqrt{34}}}{-\frac{3}{\sqrt{34}}} = \frac{5}{-3} = -\frac{5}{3}$$
The answer is $-\frac{5}{3}$. Equivalent forms accepted include -5/3, -1.667, and -1.666.
